div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
THE CATCH — why this only works for LOOKERS, not BOOKERS
(the most important finding)

div.style: color: rgb(122, 62, 18) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
A worker BOOKS a flight
(it CHANGES the world,
not just reads it)

div.style: color: rgb(107, 84, 16) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Kernel safety rule:
once the world changes, old answers
MIGHT be wrong, so the whole
whiteboard gets WIPED clean.

div.style: color: rgb(110, 31, 27) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
WHITEBOARD WIPED
everyone's saved answers gone.
Next reader pays full price again.

div.style: color: rgb(94, 61, 18) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
In a big team, ONE worker's booking
wipes the board for EVERYONE.
The lonely worker who never shares
never pays this penalty.

div.style: color: rgb(110, 31, 27) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
We measured the tipping point:
if even 1 action in 100 is a booking,
the wiping happens so often that
sharing becomes a NET LOSS.

div.style: color: rgb(27, 58, 102) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
BOTTOM LINE (read this):
* read-only fleets (research, lookups, monitoring):
sharing is a big, clean win that grows with team size.
* fleets that write a lot: sharing back-fires
because the safety-wipe is too broad.

div.style: color: rgb(28, 82, 48) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
The lead this hands us:
wipe ONLY the one answer that changed,
not the whole board (a finer eraser).
That would push the tipping point way out.
